name: Macquarie Group
lighthouseRationale: Macquarie is the AI-forward FSI benchmark for the entire APAC region — every regional bank CIO references their data platform strategy.
competitivePosture: Full Claude deployment in Commodities & Global Markets. Risk of Google expanding into adjacent divisions — executive sponsorship is the moat.

meddpicc.metrics: 40% reduction in analyst report generation time; $8M OpEx saving target in year 1
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Enterprise security posture, SOC2 Type II, model fine-tuning capability
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Signed off via IT risk committee + CFO dual approval for spend >$2M
meddpicc.paperProcess: MSA in place; annual PO cycle closes June 30
meddpicc.identifiedPain: Analyst teams spending 60% of time on report assembly vs. synthesis
meddpicc.competition: OpenAI GPT-4o (pilot in Equities); Google Gemini (evaluated, deprioritized)

name: Atlassian
lighthouseRationale: Atlassian's AI-first product posture sets the benchmark for SaaS productivity tooling across the region. Their developer community is a powerful channel.
competitivePosture: Claude integrated in Rovo AI. Renewal risk if OpenAI offers preferential pricing — maintain exec-to-exec cadence with Mike Cannon-Brookes office.

meddpicc.metrics: 25% faster Jira ticket resolution; 30% reduction in on-call escalations via Claude-in-Ops
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: API reliability, output quality on code generation, competitive pricing
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Product-led trial → VP sign-off → annual contract renewal
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual SaaS procurement cycle; Q4 renewal window
meddpicc.identifiedPain: Developers context-switching 12x/day across tools — Claude in Rovo reduces to 3x
meddpicc.competition: GitHub Copilot (incumbent on IDE); OpenAI GPT-4 (evaluated for Rovo v2)

name: Commonwealth Bank
lighthouseRationale: CBA is the dominant retail bank in Australia — their AI adoption signals direction for all four majors and regional banks.
competitivePosture: Microsoft Azure OpenAI is incumbent in retail lending. Our opening is in fraud detection and regulatory reporting where Claude's reasoning is differentiated.

meddpicc.metrics: Fraud detection accuracy improvement from 91% to 96%; AML report time cut by 50%
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: APRA compliance, data residency in Australia, model explainability for regulators
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CDAO recommendation → Group Technology Committee → Board Risk Committee
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual budget cycle; commitment needed by Oct for FY26 inclusion
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 10,000 suspicious activity reports/month; analysts can only review 30% manually
meddpicc.competition: Microsoft Azure OpenAI (incumbent); AWS Bedrock (evaluated)

name: Woodside Energy
lighthouseRationale: Woodside is the reference LNG operator for Asia-Pacific — their AI use cases in asset maintenance set the standard for the resources sector regionally.
competitivePosture: Clean run — no incumbent AI vendor. Risk is internal build preference (strong data science team). Need to land before Q3 budget freeze.

meddpicc.metrics: 12% reduction in unplanned downtime via predictive maintenance; $40M annual OPEX saving
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Ability to run locally on OT network, safety certifications, vendor longevity
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CTO steering committee evaluation Q2; decision Q3
meddpicc.paperProcess: Capital approval >$5M requires board sign-off; timing is critical
meddpicc.identifiedPain: Asset monitoring generates 4TB/day of sensor data — 90% unanalyzed
meddpicc.competition: Internal build (Woodside data science team); Palantir AIP (evaluated)

name: Telstra
lighthouseRationale: Telstra shapes Australia's digital infrastructure agenda. A Telstra win gives us credibility across the telco vertical in AU and NZ.
competitivePosture: Google Vertex AI is incumbent for network ops. Our opening is in customer service automation and internal knowledge management.

meddpicc.metrics: NPS improvement from 32 to 50 via AI customer service; $60M annual care cost reduction
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Integration with Salesforce CRM, Australian data sovereignty, multi-language SEA support
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CCO champions to board; annual tech roadmap locked Q4
meddpicc.paperProcess: Procurement lead time 4 months; target signed by August for Q4 go-live
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 2M calls/month to contact center; 40% are deflectable with AI
meddpicc.competition: Google Vertex AI (incumbent NW ops); AWS Connect (contact centre)

name: DBS Bank
lighthouseRationale: DBS is the AI benchmark for SEA banking — every regional FSI CIO references their AI-first stack. CEO Piyush Gupta is the most vocal AI advocate in Asian finance.
competitivePosture: Deep Claude deployment in wealth management and credit underwriting. Upsell opportunity in institutional trading. Guard against GPT-4o push from Piyush's US board connections.

meddpicc.metrics: AI-driven wealth advice adopted by 180K customers; credit decisioning time cut from 4hrs to 8min
meddpicc.economicBuyer: Group CEO Piyush Gupta (engaged at exec level)
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: MAS TRM compliance, explainability, model stability for credit models
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Innovation team pilots → Group CIO approval → Board Technology Committee
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual enterprise agreement; renewal Q4 FY26
meddpicc.identifiedPain: RM productivity: each relationship manager can only actively manage 80 clients; AI target is 200
meddpicc.competition: None currently active; OpenAI watching for expansion opportunity

name: Singtel
lighthouseRationale: Singtel's infrastructure reach across SEA (Optus, Airtel stake) makes them a force-multiplier win — one agreement influences subsidiaries across 5 markets.
competitivePosture: Microsoft Copilot is in internal productivity. Our target is network intelligence and the Optus enterprise customer base.

meddpicc.metrics: 30% reduction in network fault MTTR; S$200M Optus enterprise revenue unlock via AI-native services
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Regional data residency, telco-grade SLA, partner ecosystem depth
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Group CTO evaluating; Q2 recommendation to board
meddpicc.paperProcess: Enterprise agreement review annually; next window Q3 FY26
meddpicc.identifiedPain: Optus enterprise churn at 14% — AI-powered retention and upsell is the lever
meddpicc.competition: Microsoft Copilot (incumbent productivity); Google (network analytics pilot)

name: Grab Holdings
lighthouseRationale: Grab's super-app reach across SEA (500M+ touchpoints) makes them the most impactful tech lighthouse in the region. Their AI adoption is a proof point for every regional tech CIO.
competitivePosture: Claude in driver safety scoring and fraud detection. Risk: Grab has internal AI lab — retain through outcomes, not lock-in.

meddpicc.metrics: Driver safety incidents down 18%; fraud detection recall up to 98.2%
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Latency <100ms for real-time scoring, Southeast Asia data residency, model auditability
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Engineering VPs → CTO → Board (annual)
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual API agreement; credit consumption model
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 3M daily transactions requiring real-time fraud scoring at <50ms
meddpicc.competition: Internal AI lab (GrabAI); OpenAI (evaluated for GrabMaps)

name: SoftBank Group
lighthouseRationale: Masayoshi Son's endorsement of any AI company is the most powerful distribution signal in Asia. SoftBank's portfolio companies follow his AI vendor decisions.
competitivePosture: OpenAI has deep strategic partnership with SoftBank/Yahoo Japan. Our entry angle: SoftBank's global portfolio companies (ARM, WeWork recovery, T-Mobile) where we have existing relationships.

meddpicc.metrics: Not quantified — strategic partnership framing needed first
meddpicc.economicBuyer: Masayoshi Son (CEO — Son only moves on vision, not ROI decks)
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Strategic differentiation from OpenAI partnership, ability to serve portfolio companies
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Son direct decision for strategic partnerships; no committee
meddpicc.paperProcess: Non-standard — requires Anthropic CEO-level engagement
meddpicc.identifiedPain: SoftBank Vision Fund portfolio companies lack coherent AI infrastructure — Anthropic as the foundation
meddpicc.competition: OpenAI (deep strategic partner, Sam/Masa relationship); Google (Vision Fund relationship)

name: Toyota Motor Corporation
lighthouseRationale: Toyota's TPS (Toyota Production System) is the global manufacturing benchmark — their AI adoption signals where the entire manufacturing vertical is heading.
competitivePosture: Microsoft Azure is the cloud base. Our angle: Claude for engineering documentation and supplier communication across Toyota's 50,000-supplier global network.

meddpicc.metrics: Engineering doc search time reduced from 4hr to 10min; supplier onboarding cost down 30%
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Japanese language quality (must score >95% on JP-eval), on-premise option for sensitive IP
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CIO → IT Steering Committee → President's Office (for >¥1B commitments)
meddpicc.paperProcess: Toyota fiscal year April–March; budget approval by January
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 13 million engineering documents across 12 systems — engineers spend 30% of time searching
meddpicc.competition: Microsoft Azure OpenAI (incumbent); Hitachi AI (local champion relationship)

name: Samsung SDI
lighthouseRationale: Samsung SDI's battery innovation leadership makes them the R&D benchmark for manufacturing AI. A win here signals across the entire Samsung Group ecosystem.
competitivePosture: No incumbent AI vendor — greenfield opportunity. Risk of Samsung Group IT mandating an internal AI platform (Samsung Research).

meddpicc.metrics: Battery defect detection rate improvement from 99.1% to 99.8%; 6-month R&D cycle compression to 4 months
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: IP protection (Samsung IP is core competitive asset), Korean language quality, Samsung Group IT approval
meddpicc.decisionProcess: R&D VP → CTO → Samsung Group IT → CFO for >₩10B
meddpicc.paperProcess: Samsung SDI fiscal year calendar; Q3 budget lock
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 300,000 internal R&D documents — knowledge loss when senior researchers retire
meddpicc.competition: Samsung Research (internal AI mandate risk); OpenAI (evaluated for coding assist)

name: Kakao Corporation
lighthouseRationale: KakaoTalk is used by 97% of Koreans. Kakao's AI product decisions shape consumer AI adoption patterns across Korea and set precedent for the broader K-tech ecosystem.
competitivePosture: KakaoAI (internal) is incumbent. Entry through enterprise B2B services (KakaoWork, KakaoBiz) where internal LLM quality is insufficient.

meddpicc.metrics: KakaoBiz enterprise churn reduction from 18% to 9%; AI-powered B2B upsell worth ₩80B annually
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Korean language benchmark >97% on KoNLP eval, data segregation from consumer product, pricing
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Enterprise AI team → CTO → CEO (strategic calls)
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual enterprise tech budget; commitment needed Q1 for Q2 implementation
meddpicc.identifiedPain: KakaoWork enterprise customers demanding AI copilot — internal LLM underperforms on complex enterprise tasks
meddpicc.competition: KakaoAI (internal); HyperCLOVA X (NAVER — main local competitor)

name: Infosys
lighthouseRationale: Infosys as a GSI partner multiplies our reach across their 1,500+ enterprise clients globally. Their AI practice endorsement is worth 10 direct sales cycles.
competitivePosture: Microsoft is incumbent for Copilot in internal tools. Our angle is Infosys reselling Claude-powered AI services to their clients — a channel partnership, not direct replacement.

meddpicc.metrics: GSI revenue share worth $12M annually; access to 200 Infosys enterprise clients in APJ
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Partner margin structure, joint GTM commitments, model quality on enterprise code generation
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Infosys AI Center of Excellence recommendation → CGO → CEO
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual GSI agreement review; Q3 procurement window
meddpicc.identifiedPain: Clients demanding AI-native project delivery — Infosys needs a flagship LLM partner to compete with Accenture/AWS partnership
meddpicc.competition: Microsoft Azure OpenAI (incumbent, via Copilot Studio); AWS Bedrock (strategic partner eval)

name: HDFC Bank
lighthouseRationale: HDFC is India's most-watched private bank — their AI adoption in lending and customer service will set the regulatory and operational template for Indian FSI.
competitivePosture: No strong incumbent AI vendor. RBI regulatory sandbox gives us a window for a proof-of-concept before any competitor locks in.

meddpicc.metrics: Loan processing time from 7 days to 4 hours; fraud false-positive rate down 35%
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: RBI compliance, data localization (India-only servers), Hindi language quality
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CDAO → Technology Committee → MD & CEO for strategic tech
meddpicc.paperProcess: RBI sandbox approval required first; 6-month procurement cycle
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 8M monthly loan applications — 60% still manual review due to document complexity
meddpicc.competition: Temenos (incumbent core banking); AWS Bedrock (evaluated); Google Vertex (MoU signed)

name: Bank Central Asia (BCA)
lighthouseRationale: BCA processes 95% of Indonesia's inter-bank transactions — their AI stack is the de facto standard for Indonesian FSI. Win here and every regional bank follows.
competitivePosture: Google Vertex AI signed for digital banking analytics Q1. Re-engage for agentic use cases in Q4 when Google's limitations become visible.

meddpicc.metrics: Not yet defined — requires re-engagement after Google pilot evaluation Q3
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: OJK (Indonesian FSA) compliance, Bahasa Indonesia quality, onshore data
meddpicc.decisionProcess: IT Director → President Director for >IDR 50B
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual IT budget cycle locks September; re-engage by August
meddpicc.identifiedPain: Customer service handles 50M contacts/month — AI deflection target is 40%
meddpicc.competition: Google Vertex AI (just signed); AWS (evaluated Q4 2025)

name: Telkom Indonesia
lighthouseRationale: Telkom Indonesia's infrastructure monopoly means a win here touches every enterprise in Indonesia through their cloud (Telkomsel, TelkomSigma) and data center network.
competitivePosture: Microsoft Azure OpenAI is embedded in TelkomSigma's cloud offering. Our angle: Telkom's subsidiary IndiHome for consumer AI services where Microsoft isn't competing.

meddpicc.metrics: IndiHome AI service ARPU increase IDR 50K/month; TelkomSigma new revenue IDR 800B
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Bahasa Indonesia quality, government (BUMN) compliance, onshore infrastructure
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CTO → Board of Directors (BUMN procurement rules require multi-tender)
meddpicc.paperProcess: BUMN tender process: 3-month minimum; target submission Q3
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 18M IndiHome customers with zero AI services vs competitors launching AI TV/assistant
meddpicc.competition: Microsoft Azure OpenAI (TelkomSigma incumbent); Google (IndiHome pilot rumored)

name: Petronas
lighthouseRationale: Petronas is Malaysia's sovereign energy company and the most influential technology spender in the country. Their AI decisions cascade through every Malaysian GLC.
competitivePosture: No incumbent AI vendor — greenfield. Initial outreach via our Singapore team through Petronas Technology Ventures connections.

meddpicc.metrics: Not yet quantified — pre-discovery stage
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Malaysia Digital Economy framework compliance, Malay language support, sovereign AI positioning
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CTIO → Executive Leadership Team → President for strategic tech
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual capital budget cycle locks October; GLC procurement rules apply
meddpicc.identifiedPain: Upstream operations generate 2TB/day of geological data — 95% unanalyzed
meddpicc.competition: None identified yet; IBM consulting relationship (not AI-specific)

name: BDO Unibank
lighthouseRationale: BDO is the Philippines' largest bank by assets — their AI adoption will define FSI AI standards in a market of 115M people entering digital banking rapidly.
competitivePosture: No AI incumbent. BSP (central bank) is piloting AI regulatory sandbox — get in early to shape the compliance framework.

meddpicc.metrics: Not quantified — market education phase
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: BSP compliance, Filipino language support, BPO sector integration
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CTO → President → Board Technology Committee
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual IT budget; BSP sandbox approval may be prerequisite
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 10M customer service interactions/month with limited digital deflection in Filipino
meddpicc.competition: None strong yet; Microsoft exploring Copilot in financial services

name: SCB X (Siam Commercial Bank)
lighthouseRationale: SCB X is the most AI-progressive bank in Thailand and one of the most advanced in SEA — their tech subsidiary's mandate is to operate as a tech company with a banking license.
competitivePosture: Strong internal innovation culture — they built their own AI lab (Data X). Entry point: Claude API for customer-facing Thai-language applications where quality gaps exist internally.

meddpicc.metrics: Not quantified — initial discovery needed
meddpicc.economicBuyer: CEO Arthit Nanthawithaya (tech-first leader, public AI advocate)
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: Thai language quality (critical — their AI lab built Thai LLM in-house), API flexibility
meddpicc.decisionProcess: Data X team evaluation → Group CTO → CEO
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual tech investment cycle; Q3 commitment target
meddpicc.identifiedPain: Customer-facing AI products limited by Thai language model quality — internal model underperfoms on complex queries
meddpicc.competition: Data X internal (SCB's own AI lab); Google Vertex (evaluated for translation)

name: ANZ Bank NZ
lighthouseRationale: ANZ NZ's AI adoption signals direction for all four major NZ banks. Their parent ANZ Group (AU) creates a natural expansion path from our Australian wins.
competitivePosture: No AI incumbent. Leverage ANZ Group AU relationship to land a group-level agreement that covers NZ division.

meddpicc.metrics: Credit decisioning time from 2 days to 2 hours; NPS improvement of +8 points
meddpicc.decisionCriteria: RBNZ compliance, data residency in NZ, synergy with ANZ Group AU agreement
meddpicc.decisionProcess: CIO recommendation → CEO → ANZ Group CIO sign-off
meddpicc.paperProcess: Annual IT budget; Q3 lock for calendar year planning
meddpicc.identifiedPain: 600K retail customers; 40% of credit applications still manually reviewed
